perturbation.original_text: Pursuant and subject to Rule 13d-1(k)(1) promulgated under the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, as amended, the undersigned hereby agree to the joint filing of the Statement on Schedule 13D to which this Joint Filing Agreement is attached, and any amendments thereto may be filed without the necessity of filing additional joint filing agreements.
perturbation.changed_text: Pursuant and subject to Rule 13d-1(k)(1) promulgated under the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, as amended, the undersigned hereby agree to the joint filing of the Statement on Schedule 13D to which this Joint Filing Agreement is attached. Any amendments thereto must be filed with new joint filing agreements signed by all parties.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction by stating that amendments to Schedule 13D *must* be filed with new joint filing agreements signed by all parties. This directly contradicts the original text's assertion that amendments may be filed without the necessity of additional joint filing agreements, creating ambiguity about the required procedure for filing amendments. This uncertainty may affect the enforcement of filing obligations, leading to disputes over compliance.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Pursuant and subject to Rule 13d-1(k)(1) promulgated under the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, as amended, the undersigned hereby agree to the joint filing of the Statement on Schedule 13D to which this Joint Filing Agreement is attached. Any amendments thereto must be filed with new joint filing agreements signed by all parties. This Joint Filing Agreement may be executed in any number of counterparts, each of which shall be deemed an original, but all of which shall constitute one and the same instrument. The execution and filing of this agreement shall not be construed as an admission that the below-named parties are a group or have acted as a group. perturbation.justification: The changed text mandates that all amendments to Schedule 13D *must* be filed with new joint filing agreements signed by all parties. This directly contradicts the original text which stated that amendments 'may be filed without the necessity of filing additional joint filing agreements'. This inconsistency creates uncertainty about the required procedure for filing amendments, potentially leading to disputes over compliance and enforceability. The contradicted text is simply a re-statement of the changed text, solidifying that there is an internal contradiction created in the text by this amendment.

perturbation.original_text: This Joint Filing Agreement may be executed in any number of counterparts, each of which shall be deemed an original, but all of which shall constitute one and the same instrument.
perturbation.changed_text: This Joint Filing Agreement must be executed in a single original document, and counterparts are not permitted.
perturbation.explanation: This change directly contradicts the previous sentence by stating that the agreement *must* be executed in a single original document, disallowing counterparts, whereas the original text explicitly permits counterparts. This contradiction creates uncertainty regarding the validity of the agreement if executed in multiple counterpart documents. This could invalidate the agreement depending on which part is enforceable, leading to potential legal disputes.

This directly contradicts the paragraph in the Contradicted Text which also says 'This Joint Filing Agreement must be executed in a single original document, and counterparts are not permitted.' However, it strongly contradicts the original provision allowing for counterparts, thereby creating an in-text contradiction within the overall agreement as highlighted by the Perturbation Explanation.

perturbation.original_text: The execution and filing of this agreement shall not be construed as an admission that the below-named parties are a group or have acted as a group.
perturbation.changed_text: The execution and filing of this agreement shall be construed as an acknowledgement that the below-named parties are acting as a group.
perturbation.explanation: This perturbation creates a direct contradiction within the text. Originally, the agreement states that its execution and filing *shall not* be construed as an admission of the parties acting as a group. The modified text states that the same action *shall* be construed as an acknowledgement that the parties are acting as a group. This in-text contradiction regarding whether the agreement implies group action introduces significant legal uncertainty. This is because, whether the filling of this agreement means or not the parties are acting as a group has huge legal impact.
